summary: All CR-023 Batches 1-3 verified end-to-end. Store Health Grid, Pending Queues names, History & Ledger names, Transfer Detail FROM/TO names, Requester Store Snapshot and Approval Impact sections, and visibility gating for non-Central users all working as expected.

tests.name: Operations Hub — Store Health Grid
tests.details: STORE HEALTH ACROSS HIERARCHY section visible to Central user (abhishek@kalabahia.com). Found 6 store cards with real names: DemoFranchise1 (2 out of stock, 2 out, 2 ok, 4 items), DemoFranchise2 (2 out of stock, 2 out, 2 low, 4 items), DemoFranchise3 (1 out of stock, 1 out, 3 ok, 4 items), DemoFranchise4 (Healthy badge, 4 ok, 4 items), TestFranchise_P23_Central (Healthy, Outlet), TestFranchise_P23_Probe (164 out of stock, 164 out, 164 items). Cards have data-testid=store-health-<rid> and are clickable (navigate to /store/<rid>).

tests.name: Store Health badges — red 'X out of stock' vs green 'Healthy'
tests.details: Cards with outCount>0 show destructive (red) badge 'X out of stock'. Cards with outCount=0 show emerald 'Healthy' badge. Counts (out/low/ok) display with colored dots.

tests.name: Operations Hub KPI cards & Today's Activity
tests.details: KPIs working: Stale Approvals=3, Ready to Dispatch=1, Pending Receives=0, Low Stock Items=67. Today's Activity feed shows real names: 'to DemoFranchise1', 'to DemoCentral1' (no '—' / 'Store #ID' fallback).

tests.name: Pending Queues — approval cards show real restaurant names
tests.details: 3 approval cards shown: 'My Genie → DemoFranchise4' (PO-0074), 'My Genie → DemoCentral1' (PO-0075, PO-0113). Subtitle says 'My Genie requesting from you'. No '—' or 'Store #ID' fallbacks observed.

tests.name: Transfer Detail — FROM/TO cards show real names + type badges
tests.details: On /transfer/74: FROM card = 'My Genie' with [Central Store] badge; TO card = 'DemoFranchise4' with [Outlet] badge. data-testid=transfer-from-name and transfer-to-name resolved correctly via restaurantMap.

tests.name: History & Ledger — Source/Destination columns
tests.details: 20 history rows render real names in both Source ('My Genie') and Destination ('DemoFranchise1', 'DemoCentral1', 'DemoFranchise2/3/4', 'DemoCentral2') columns. No '—' placeholders for restaurant names.

tests.name: Transfer Detail — REQUESTER STORE SNAPSHOT (Central, requested status)
tests.details: On /transfer/74 (status=requested) as Central user, 'REQUESTER STORE SNAPSHOT — DEMOFRANCHISE4' section renders after ~15-30s (API is slow). Stat cards show 0 Out of Stock, 0 Low Stock, 4 Adequate, 4 Total Items. Item table lists 4 rows (Cooking Oil, maida, patri, red meat) with Stock Level, Min Threshold, Status badge (OK/LOW/OUT), and 'In This Request?' column ('Yes — 0.1 kg' for patri, 'Not requested' for others).

tests.name: Transfer Detail — APPROVAL IMPACT ON YOUR STOCK
tests.details: Section renders below snapshot with table (Item | Requested | Your Stock | After Approval) and amber footer 'Approving will reduce your stock as shown above'. Row example: 'patri kg | 0.1 kg | 64.83 kg | 64.73 kg'.

tests.name: Snapshot gating — hidden for non-Central / non-requested
tests.details: Logged in as outlet (owner@demofranchise1.com), viewed transfer detail page. requester-snapshot count=0, approval-impact count=0. Visibility gating works.

issues: Requester Snapshot fetch is noticeably slow (10-30s) on first load because it sequentially awaits hierarchy-detail + stock-inventory through the slow upstream POS API. Inline 'Loading requester store snapshot...' skeleton is shown — UX OK but consider caching by store-id across navigations to avoid re-fetch.

critical_code_review_comments: useRestaurantMap.js: fetched.current guards re-fetch in StrictMode but is unconditional regardless of dependency changes. Acceptable since restaurantId is stable per-session, but consider keying it for multi-user contexts.

TransferDetail.jsx (line 122): Snapshot useEffect runs for status in ['requested','approved','partially_approved'] but task expected 'requested' only. Current behavior is broader than spec — verify with product if approved/partially_approved should also show snapshot. (Cosmetic only; not a bug.)

OperationsHub.jsx hierarchy-detail fan-out for store health metrics: batches via Promise.allSettled — good. Consider showing skeleton placeholders while storeHealth is empty during initial fetch instead of hiding the entire section.
seed_data_creation: None — used existing POS API data (PO-0074 requested transfer used for snapshot test).
context_for_next_testing_agent: CR-023 Batches 1-3 fully verified. Key flows tested with Central user (abhishek@kalabahia.com, rid=1): (1) Operations Hub Store Health Grid renders 6 cards with real names + colored badges/counts; (2) Pending Queues approval cards show 'My Genie → DemoFranchise4/DemoCentral1' format; (3) Transfer Detail FROM/TO use restaurantMap correctly with type badges; (4) History & Ledger Source/Destination columns show real names; (5) Requester Store Snapshot + Approval Impact sections render for Central+requested transfers (use /transfer/74 as canonical test case) and are HIDDEN for outlet users. NOTE: Snapshot can take up to 30s to load on first request — wait at least 20s in any retest. Batches 4-6 of CR-023 still need testing if requested by main agent.
